Action: Concurred by GMS Comment:
Action Taken By: Joi Grymes-Johnson
Action Taken On: 8/28/2024 2:01:03 PM ET
The information provided within the application adheres to the Notice of Funding Opportunity.
Completeness and Eligibility Checklist
As of 02/12/2026 01:43:54 PM
1. Did the applicant include a Project Narrative? [ X ] Yes [_] No
2. New applicant with a co-applicant board: Does the application include Attachment 6: Co-Applicant Agreement?
[ _] Yes [ _] No [ X ] N/A
3. New applicant: Does the application include Attachment 11: Evidence of Nonprofit or Public Center Status?
[ _] Yes [ _] No [ X ] N/A
4. New applicant: Is the applicant a private, nonprofit entity or a public agency (including Tribal, and urban Indian organizations) in the United States? Suggested Resource(s): Attachment 11: Evidence of Nonprofit or Public Center Status [" :selected:
[ _] Yes [ _ ] No [ X ] N/A
5. Does the applicant propose to operate a health center that makes all required primary health care services available and accessible in the service area, either directly or through established arrangements, without regard for ability to pay? (Note that applicants may not propose to provide ONLY a single service or any subset of the required primary health care services.)
Suggested Resource(s): Project Narrative[] | Form 5A: Services Provided[" [ X ] Yes [_] No
6. Does the applicant propose to make General Primary Medical Care available directly (Column I) and/or through formal written contractual agreements in which the health center pays for the service (Column II)?
Suggested Resource(s): Form 5A: Services Provided [" [ X ] Yes [_ ] No
7. Does the applicant propose to perform a substantive role in the project (i.e., does not apply on behalf of another organization)? Suggested Resource(s): Budget Narrative[? | Attachment 2: Bylaws[] | Form 5A: Services Provided[" | Form 8: Health Center Agreements[." [ X ] Yes [ _] No
8. Does the applicant propose to make services accessible to all? If a sub-population is targeted (e.g., a school-based site that targets school-aged children), will health center services be available and accessible to others seeking services at the proposed site(s)?
Suggested Resource(s): Project Narrative[?
[ X ] Yes [_] No
https://grants2.hrsa.gov/WebGAM2Internal/Interface/PrintForm.aspx?RV=5ae54e52-3321-44d5-b89a-87f6cb33ed25&RTC=19&PrintPage=False&IsPo ...

2/12/26, 1:44 PM
Print All | RO | HRSA EHBs
9. Does the applicant propose a service area announced under this NOFO number? If the answer to Question 9 is "No", select "N/A" for Questions 10 through 13.
Suggested Resource(s): Summary Pager? | SAAT[] | Project Abstract[" | Project Narrativer? | Form 5B: Service Sites["
[ X ] Yes [_ ] No
10. Is the total number of unduplicated patients projected to be served in 2026 on Form 1A at least 75 percent of the SAAT Patient Target? Suggested Resource(s): SAAT[? | Form 1A: General Information Worksheetr?
[ X ] Yes [ _ ] No [_] N/A
11. Does the applicant propose to serve patients for each Service Type listed in the SAAT? Suggested Resource(s): SAATE" | Form 1A: General Information Worksheet["
[ X ] Yes [ _ ] No [_ ] N/A
12. Does the applicant request annual SAC funding that does NOT exceed the SAAT Total Funding for the service area?
Suggested Resource(s): SF-424A[" | Budget Narrative[? | SAATE"
[ X ] Yes [ _ ] No [_] N/A
13. Does the applicant maintain the current funding distribution (i.e., CHC, MHC, HCH, PHPC) from the SAAT? Suggested Resource(s): SF-424AL | SAATE
[ X ] Yes [ _ ] No [_]N/A
14. New or competing supplement applicant: For Form 5B: Service Sites, does the applicant include: Service area zip codes where the zip code patient percentages on the SAAT total at least 75 percent; or All service area zip codes if the sum of all zip code patient percentages on the SAAT is less than 75 percent?
Suggested Resource(s): Form 5B: Service Sites[? | SAATE"
[ _] Yes [ _] No [ X ] N/A
15. New or competing supplement applicant: Is at least one new full-time (operational 40 hours or more per week) permanent, fixed building site with a verifiable street address proposed on Form 5B? If funding is only requested for MHC, the applicant may propose a new full-time seasonal (rather than permanent) service delivery site.
Suggested Resource(s): Form 5B: Service Sites["
[ _] Yes [ _] No [ X ] N/A
16. New or competing supplement applicant requesting PHPC funding: Does the applicant demonstrate consultation with residents of public housing in the preparation of the SAC application and ensure ongoing consultation with the residents regarding the planning and administration of the health center? Suggested Resource(s): Project Narrative [?
[ _] Yes [ _] No [ X ] N/A
17. New or competing supplement applicant requesting HCH and/or PHPC funding: Does the applicant attest on the Summary Page that funding will be utilized to supplement, and not supplant, the expenditures of the health center and the value of in-kind contributions for the delivery of services to these populations? Suggested Resource(s): Summary Pager?
[ _] Yes [ _] No [ X ] N/A
Patient Projection Funding Reduction Check
As of 02/12/2026 01:43:56 PM
If the Form 1A patient projection is less than 95% of the SAAT Patient Target, is the annual SAC funding request reduced accordingly in the SF-424A and Budget Narrative? * This question does not impact application completeness or eligibility.

lines.content: 00231472: AMISTAD COMMUNITY HEALTH CENTER, INCORPORATED, Corpus Christi, TX
The information provided within the application adheres to the Notice of Funding Opportunity.
1. Did the applicant include a Project Narrative?
2. New applicant with a co-applicant board: Does the application include Attachment 6: Co-Applicant Agreement?
3. New applicant: Does the application include Attachment 11: Evidence of Nonprofit or Public Center Status?
4. New applicant: Is the applicant a private, nonprofit entity or a public agency (including Tribal, and urban Indian organizations) in the United States?
Suggested Resource(s): Attachment 11: Evidence of Nonprofit or Public Center Status ["
5. Does the applicant propose to operate a health center that makes all required primary health care services available and accessible in the service area, either
directly or through established arrangements, without regard for ability to pay? (Note that applicants may not propose to provide ONLY a single service or any
subset of the required primary health care services.)
Suggested Resource(s): Project Narrative[] | Form 5A: Services Provided["
6. Does the applicant propose to make General Primary Medical Care available directly (Column I) and/or through formal written contractual agreements in which
the health center pays for the service (Column II)?
7. Does the applicant propose to perform a substantive role in the project (i.e., does not apply on behalf of another organization)?
Suggested Resource(s): Budget Narrative[? | Attachment 2: Bylaws[] | Form 5A: Services Provided[" | Form 8: Health Center Agreements[."
8. Does the applicant propose to make services accessible to all? If a sub-population is targeted (e.g., a school-based site that targets school-aged children), will
health center services be available and accessible to others seeking services at the proposed site(s)?
9. Does the applicant propose a service area announced under this NOFO number? If the answer to Question 9 is "No", select "N/A" for Questions 10 through
Suggested Resource(s): Summary Pager? | SAAT[] | Project Abstract[" | Project Narrativer? | Form 5B: Service Sites["
10. Is the total number of unduplicated patients projected to be served in 2026 on Form 1A at least 75 percent of the SAAT Patient Target?
Suggested Resource(s): SAAT[? | Form 1A: General Information Worksheetr?
11. Does the applicant propose to serve patients for each Service Type listed in the SAAT?
Suggested Resource(s): SAATE" | Form 1A: General Information Worksheet["
12. Does the applicant request annual SAC funding that does NOT exceed the SAAT Total Funding for the service area?
Suggested Resource(s): SF-424A[" | Budget Narrative[? | SAATE"
13. Does the applicant maintain the current funding distribution (i.e., CHC, MHC, HCH, PHPC) from the SAAT?
14. New or competing supplement applicant: For Form 5B: Service Sites, does the applicant include: Service area zip codes where the zip code patient
percentages on the SAAT total at least 75 percent; or All service area zip codes if the sum of all zip code patient percentages on the SAAT is less than 75
Suggested Resource(s): Form 5B: Service Sites[? | SAATE"
15. New or competing supplement applicant: Is at least one new full-time (operational 40 hours or more per week) permanent, fixed building site with a verifiable
street address proposed on Form 5B? If funding is only requested for MHC, the applicant may propose a new full-time seasonal (rather than permanent) service
16. New or competing supplement applicant requesting PHPC funding: Does the applicant demonstrate consultation with residents of public housing in the
preparation of the SAC application and ensure ongoing consultation with the residents regarding the planning and administration of the health center?
17. New or competing supplement applicant requesting HCH and/or PHPC funding: Does the applicant attest on the Summary Page that funding will be utilized
to supplement, and not supplant, the expenditures of the health center and the value of in-kind contributions for the delivery of services to these populations?
If the Form 1A patient projection is less than 95% of the SAAT Patient Target, is the annual SAC funding request reduced accordingly in the SF-424A and Budget
Narrative? * This question does not impact application completeness or eligibility.
Suggested Resource(s): SF-424A[" | Budget Narrative[] | SAAT[" | Patient and Budget Calculator[."
Is the application complete based on the required documents mentioned in the Completeness and Eligibility Checklist?
Based on BPHC OPPD review, is the applicant eligible?
